model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Switching from Adam to decoupled AdamW with slightly stronger weight decay often improves validation loss for short-budget CNN training by preserving optimization speed while regularizing more effectively."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"A small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity regularizer that often improves CIFAR-10 validation loss under short training budgets by reducing overconfident logits."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The prior run regressed substantially with label smoothing, so removing it is the safest simplification to recover the stronger loss level before testing new ideas."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The current model is very small; a moderate width increase in convolutional and head layers should improve representational capacity within the same fixed-step budget with minimal code complexity."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last gain came from added capacity, so a further moderate width increase is the most direct continuation and may reduce underfitting within the same step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Capacity scaling has consistently helped, and increasing stage expansion ratio is a simple way to add representational power without changing training logic."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"After several capacity increases, a slightly lower peak learning rate can improve stability and final loss under the same cosine schedule and fixed-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The reduced learning rate increased validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the stronger optimization setting for this fixed-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.05 * total_steps))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"With only 256 steps, shortening warmup from 10% to 5% should spend more of the budget near useful learning rates while keeping the same stable cosine decay."}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.05 * total_steps))","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Shorter warmup regressed validation loss, so restoring the previous 10% warmup is the safest recovery before testing other directions."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"After increasing model capacity, adding light dropout is a simple regularization change that can improve validation loss without altering the training loop."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"Dropout caused a large regression, so reverting to zero dropout restores the strongest validated configuration before exploring further changes."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"With higher model capacity and no dropout, slightly reducing AdamW weight decay may improve fit while retaining regularization under the fixed training budget."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Lowering weight decay slightly worsened validation loss, so reverting to 5e-4 returns to the stronger regularization setting."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"A deeper feature extractor may yield better hierarchical representations on CIFAR-10; testing depth increase is the most direct remaining architecture lever with minimal code complexity."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"After increasing depth, a slightly lower learning rate can stabilize optimization for the larger model and may improve final validation loss within the same fixed step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The lower learning rate regressed this deeper model, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the stronger optimization setting."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"With a very short effective training horizon, reducing AdamW's second beta can make variance estimates adapt faster and may improve early optimization and final validation loss."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Lowering AdamW beta2 regressed validation loss, so restoring the original beta setting returns to the strongest validated optimizer dynamics."}
